title: Ancient Roman Temple Found Under a School in Frankfurt
summary: In Frankfurt, Germany, archaeologists found an old Roman temple while building a new school. This temple is very special because it was hidden underground for a long time. It has stone buildings, pits, and shafts where people long ago left offerings like pottery, coins, and animal bones. Scientists think these were gifts to gods during rituals. They also found clues that might mean people were sacrificed there, which is very unusual for Roman times. A big team of experts from museums and universities will study the site for three years with over one million euros in funding. This discovery helps us learn about Roman life and religion in Germany. The temple shows how the past and future connect, as it was found during school construction.

a special building where people worship gods
gifts left for gods, like food or coins
special ceremonies or actions people do for religious reasons
giving up something important, sometimes an animal or person, to please gods

questions.correct_answer: It teaches us about Roman life and religion

background_read: The Roman Empire was a powerful ancient civilization that ruled many parts of Europe, including Germany, about 2,000 years ago. Romans built cities, temples, and roads, and they had gods they worshipped with rituals. Archaeologists are like detectives who dig up old sites to learn how people lived long ago. In Frankfurt, a city in Germany, they found a Roman temple while building a new school. This temple has pits where Romans left offerings like pottery and animal bones, which might have been part of religious ceremonies. Sometimes, in ancient times, people did sacrifices, which means giving up something valuable to gods, and this temple might have clues about that. Scientists from museums and universities will study it to understand more about Roman history and how it connects to today's world.
Article_Structure: WHO: Archaeologists and experts from museums and universities in Germany and Switzerland.
WHAT: A Roman temple with buildings, pits, and offerings found under a school in Frankfurt.
WHERE: Frankfurt, Germany, in the ancient city of Nida.
WHY: To learn about Roman rituals, offerings, and possible sacrifices, and to connect past and future through research.

perspectives.description: The temple is a rare find that helps us understand Roman religion and daily life in ancient Germany.
